---
title: "restaurant"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(osmdata)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tigris)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r}

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# Step 1: Get Maryland counties (as sf)
md_counties <- counties(state = "MD", cb = TRUE, class = "sf")
print("Loaded Maryland counties:")
print(md_counties$NAME)

# Step 2: Query OSM for restaurants within the bounding box of MD counties
bbox_md <- st_bbox(md_counties)

q <- opq(bbox = bbox_md) %>%
  add_osm_feature(key = "amenity", value = "restaurant")

osm_restaurants <- osmdata_sf(q)

# Step 3: Extract only the point features (most restaurants are mapped as points)
restaurant_points <- osm_restaurants$osm_points

# Inspect what we got
cat("Number of restaurant points downloaded:", nrow(restaurant_points), "\n")
print(head(restaurant_points[, c("name", "amenity")]))

# Step 4: Make sure CRS matches — transform counties to match restaurant points
md_counties <- st_transform(md_counties, st_crs(restaurant_points))

# Step 5: Spatial join — assign each restaurant point to a county
restaurant_with_county <- st_join(restaurant_points, md_counties, join = st_within)

# Step 6: Remove rows with NA values in the county name (if any restaurants don't match)
restaurant_with_county_clean <- restaurant_with_county %>%
  filter(!is.na(NAME))

# Check how many rows are left after cleaning
cat("Number of restaurants after removing NA county values:", nrow(restaurant_with_county_clean), "\n")

# Step 7: Count restaurants per county (now that we've removed NA counties)
restaurant_counts <- restaurant_with_county_clean %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(NAME) %>%
  summarise(restaurant_count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(restaurant_count))

# Step 8: Print result
print("Restaurant counts by county:")
print(restaurant_counts)

# Step 9 (Optional): Visualize as a bar chart
ggplot(restaurant_counts, aes(x = reorder(NAME, -restaurant_count), y = restaurant_count)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4682B4") +
  labs(title = "Number of Restaurants per County in Maryland (from OSM)",
       x = "County", y = "Number of Restaurants") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
